name: Nevatim Air Base
hardening: Extensive hardened aircraft shelters; underground command facilities
description: IAF's premier strike base in the Negev desert. Primary home of Israel's F-35I Adir stealth fleet — spearhead of any operation against Iranian nuclear facilities. Significant expansion and hardening in recent years.

name: Ramon Air Base
description: IAF's southernmost operational base in the Negev near Eilat. Hosts F-16I Sufa long-range strike squadrons and the IAF Flight Academy. Closest base to the Red Sea corridor — a potential route for long-range strikes toward Iran.

name: Ramat David Air Base
hardening: Hardened aircraft shelters; buried command bunkers
description: IAF's primary northern fighter base in the Jezreel Valley near Haifa. F-16 and Apache units oriented toward the northern front against Hezbollah in Lebanon. Within range of Hezbollah rocket threats — key operational hub during past Lebanon conflicts.

name: Tel Nof Air Base
hardening: Extensive hardened shelters; underground operations center
description: One of IAF's largest installations in central Israel. Home base of F-15I Ra'am long-range strike fighters — primary platform for deep-strike missions critical in any Iran operation. Also hosts heavy-lift helicopter and UAV units.

name: Palmachim Air Base
hardening: Hardened missile silos and launch facilities
description: Israel's primary missile test and space launch facility on the Mediterranean coast. Home of Arrow BMD system. Believed associated with Jericho nuclear-capable ballistic missiles — key element of Israel's strategic deterrent. Also Israel's spaceport for Shavit reconnaissance satellite launches.

name: HaKirya (IDF HQ)
hardening: Deeply buried underground command center ('The Pit'/HaBor); blast-resistant structures
description: IDF's central military headquarters in the heart of Tel Aviv. Houses General Staff, Military Intelligence (Aman), and Ministry of Defense. The deeply buried underground 'Pit' serves as Israel's national military nerve center for directing any large-scale operation.

name: Haifa Naval Base
hardening: Hardened submarine pens; protected port infrastructure
description: Israeli Navy's primary operational base in Haifa. Home to Dolphin-class submarines believed capable of launching nuclear-armed cruise missiles as Israel's sea-based second-strike deterrent. Also hosts modern Sa'ar 6 corvettes with Barak-8 and Iron Dome naval variant.

name: Dimona Nuclear Research Center
hardening: Multi-level underground complex (est. 6+ levels); Patriot and Arrow air defense umbrella
description: Israel's primary nuclear weapons production facility in the Negev desert. Houses IRR-2 reactor and multi-story underground plutonium reprocessing plant. Core of Israel's undeclared nuclear program (est. 80-400 warheads). One of the most heavily defended sites in Israel with dedicated air defense batteries and strict no-fly zone.
